---
name: "Miguel"
surname: "Lara-Rangel"
linkedin: jmlr
github: joskelar

info:
  - "I am a PhD student working on uncertainty-aware AI for informed decision-making, with a focus on applications in climate and finance. I have several years of experience implementing and leading applied AI projects across banking, financial risk, and pharma, including work on statistical model governance and model risk (regulation). In parallel, I like to contribute to initiatives that expand AI literacy, education, and policy capacity in emerging markets."

interests:
  - Foundational AI and learning theory
  - Uncertainty quantification and probabilistic machine learning
  - Model risk management, AI regulation and accountability
  - AI for climate and environmental decision-making
  - Quantitative finance and risk management
  - Philosophy and history

publications:
  - authors:
      - J. M. Lara-Rangel
      - et al.
    title: "Exploring Pseudo-Token Approaches in Transformer Neural Processes"
    year: 2025
    journal: "AABI 2025"
  - authors:
      - J. M. Lara-Rangel
      - et al.
    title: "Learning to Forget with Hypernetworks"
    year: 2024
    journal: "AdvML-Frontiers @ NeurIPS 2024"
  - authors:
      - J. M. Lara-Rangel
      - et al.
    title: "On the Limits of Applying Graph Transformers for Brain Connectome Classification"
    year: 2025
    journal: "ICBINB @ ICLR 2025"

education:
  - institution: University of Cambridge
    location: Cambridge, UK
    degree: MPhil in Machine Learning and Machine Intelligence
    startdate: 2023
    enddate: 2024
  - institution: Benemérita Universidad Autónoma de Puebla
    location: Puebla, Mexico
    degree: BSc in Actuarial Science
    startdate: 2016
    enddate: 2020

---

{{< academic-webpage >}}
